title: Generated Non Python Maturity Labels
goal: Resolve #408 by making generated non-Python package maturity labels explicit and protected by generated proof.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.outcome: Reviewers and weak agents can distinguish generated TypeScript proof fixtures from runnable read-only adapters from generated package output and checks.
intent.constraints: Do not build new TypeScript runtime primitives, require Node or Docker for ordinary Python development, or hand-edit generated outputs.
intent.latitude: Tighten source IR/check/generator/tests and regenerate derived package outputs.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.next_step: Archive this plan, update external issue evidence, and close #408 with proof.
execution.proof: Run generated command package checks, focused contract tests, workspace tests, ruff, planning doctor, summary, and reconcile.

invariants: Generated files are regenerated from the command-package IR.
Proof fixtures stay non-runnable and forbidden for weak-agent routing.
Runnable read-only adapters remain review-required until weak-agent-safe criteria are proven.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Keep generated non-Python command packages portable and useful without letting proof fixtures masquerade as weak-agent-safe CLI surfaces.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Generated TypeScript package tests and static checks now prove maturity claims and weak-agent routing status from generated metadata.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Source-level generator/checks, regenerated TypeScript package tests, Node conformance, and Docker package tests passed.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Refresh summary and promote another explicit child slice from the weak-agent recovery lane.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make generated non-Python package maturity explicit and reviewable.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: No new runtime primitives, no ordinary-development dependency on Node or Docker, no manual edits to generated outputs.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact generated test and static-check assertions that prove maturity labels and weak-agent routing.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

active_milestone.scope: Command-package IR, generator, generated TypeScript package metadata/tests, static check, and focused contract tests.

immediate_next_action: Archive this plan, update external issue evidence, and close #408 with proof.
invariants: Generated files are regenerated from the command-package IR.
Proof fixtures stay non-runnable and forbidden for weak-agent routing.
Runnable read-only adapters remain review-required until weak-agent-safe criteria are proven.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q -k "command_package or generated_typescript"
uv run ruff check src tests scripts
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Generated TypeScript package tests assert maturity id, generation status, promotion criteria, runnable state, fixtureOnly state, and weak-agent routing.
Static generated-package checks reject missing maturity vocabulary or weak-agent routing mismatches.
Generated package output remains fresh from the command-package IR.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Generated TypeScript package tests now assert generation status, maturity id, promotion criteria, runnable state, fixtureOnly state, weak-agent routing, and bin presence/absence; static checks reject missing maturity vocabulary or weak-agent routing mismatches.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q -k "command_package or generated_typescript"; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --conformance --require-node; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --docker --require-docker; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run ruff check src tests scripts
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: none for #408; parent weak-agent recovery lane remains open through sibling issues.
execution_summary.resume from: Refresh summary and select the next explicit child slice.

proof_report.validation proof: All selected generated-package, conformance, Docker, workspace, lint, and planning proof commands passed.
proof_report.proof achieved now: Generated non-Python package outputs state maturity and weak-agent routing in package metadata, generated tests assert that status, and the static checker protects the source maturity vocabulary.

discard.summary: No full TypeScript runtime primitive implementation is needed for #408.

continuation.summary: none for #408; parent weak-agent recovery lane remains open through sibling issues.
